description_long: This is a medieval-style helmet made of dark, metallic material, featuring a pronounced central ridge running from the top to the back. The front has a sharply angled visor with two narrow, triangular eye slits, giving it an intimidating, aggressive appearance. The sides are adorned with circular, rosette-like hinge covers, suggesting the visor may be movable. The surface is slightly matte with subtle texturing, indicating wear or forging marks. The helmet fully encloses the head, with a pointed crest at the top and a flared lower edge to protect the neck. There are no visible modern features such as branding, graphics, or ventilation holes.
description: A dark, medieval-style full-face helmet with a pointed crest, angular visor, and narrow eye slits, made of metal with a matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed crest, sharp visor, and two narrow, triangular eye slits.
description_view_1: Left three-quarter view highlights the rounded dome, side hinge detail, and the angular shape of the visor.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the pronounced central ridge and the smooth, rounded back of the helmet.
description_view_3: Right three-quarter view mirrors the left, showing the hinge and the visor's angular profile.
